question.label: Wait too long
question.name: When you hold back on asking, what are you usually unsure about specifically?
question.description: Drills into the exact uncertainty behind his delayed asks so the manager can remove that friction.

answer: When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.

assessment.note: Names a missing condition — specific, fast feedback — as what unlocks his pace, which signals both unclear standards and insufficient growth support; [BUDGET-STARVED]

new_queue.label: Thread follow
new_queue.name: feedback specific fast, — can you say more about what that means for you right now?

new_queue.label: Go-to people
new_queue.name: Who have you found easiest to go to when you get stuck?

new_queue.label: Next support
new_queue.name: What support from me would help you move faster this fortnight?

history.answer_excerpt: I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
Local setup is better now, but I still lose time when the design and API details do not match.
I am not always sure who should approve what before I push.
I usually wait too long before asking because I want to solve things myself first.
When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.
When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.
